name: Mexican Pizza with Salsa
recipeIngredient: Basic bread dough (1/2 recipe, risen once)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F with the rack in the lower area of the oven.
2. Oil a round pizza pan, preferably black steel, and sprinkle lightly with cornmeal. Set aside.
3. Roll the dough into a circle about 11 inches in diameter. If the dough is still retracting, let it relax for a few minutes before continuing.
4. Place the rolled dough in the prepared pan and gently stretch it to fit the size of the pan. Prick the dough with a fork.
5. Heat oil in a 7-inch skillet over medium-high heat.
6. Remove the casing from the sausage and crumble the meat into 1/2-inch pieces.
7. Add the sausage, garlic, and onion to the hot oil. Cook until the onion is softened, about 4 minutes, stirring often.
8. Spread the sausage mixture onto the dough, leaving a 1/2-inch margin around the edges.
9. Sprinkle half of the cheese over the sausage mixture.
10. Bake in the preheated oven for 12 minutes.
11. Sprinkle the remaining cheese over the pizza.
12. Continue baking until the cheese has melted and the crust is deeply browned, about 3 more minutes.
13. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with minced cilantro and sliced green onions.
14. Cut into wedges and garnish each wedge with drained salsa and cilantro leaves. Serve hot.
